title: DISTINCT Values
description: Remove pesky duplicates from your results.

slides.title: A sea of duplicates 👯
slides.content:
# The Copycat Problem

Imagine an app with 1,000 users. If you run `SELECT country FROM users;`, you'll get a massive list of 1,000 countries.

But what if 800 people are from 'USA'? Your results will literally just say 'USA' 800 times. That's not very helpful if you just want to know *which* countries your app is used in.

slides.title: Filtering with DISTINCT ✨
slides.content:
## Sifting out the copies

You can tell the database to automatically remove all duplicates by placing the word **`DISTINCT`** right after SELECT.

`SELECT DISTINCT country FROM users;`

quizData.question: What is the primary function of the DISTINCT keyword?

options.text: It finds the single highest value in a column
It automatically strips away all duplicate values in your search results
It deletes duplicate rows hidden inside the database

quizData.explanation: DISTINCT filters your output to only show unique values. It does not actually delete any data from the database.

slides.title: Combining Columns 🤝
slides.content:
## Distinct Combinations

If you use `DISTINCT` on multiple columns, it looks for unique *combinations*.

`SELECT DISTINCT first_name, last_name FROM employees;`

If you have two "John Smiths", it only shows one. But if you have "John Smith" and "John Doe", it shows both because the combo is different.

quizData.question: If I run `SELECT DISTINCT make, color FROM cars;`, and there are three Red Fords and one Blue Ford, what will I see?

options.text: One 'Red Ford' row and one 'Blue Ford' row

quizData.explanation: Because you used DISTINCT on both columns, it returns every unique combination of make and color.

slides.title: Write it!
slides.content: Show me all the unique, distinct genres of movies in our `movies` table.
